 {"id":"bd-3nhw","title":"Server module async integration tests (shutdown drain, force-close, phase_changed)","description":"Write async integration tests for the server module (src/server/) covering:
1. Graceful shutdown completes in-flight requests (spawn connections, begin_drain, verify wait_all_closed resolves)
2. Force close after drain timeout (begin_drain, sleep past deadline, begin_force_close)
3. phase_changed notification (await phase transitions)
4. Concurrent connection registration during drain (verify rejection)
5. ShutdownStats collection

These test the async paths that the unit tests in shutdown.rs and connection.rs cannot cover. Follows from asupersync-z8n implementation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:15:25.924213608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T17:21:25.299143910Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T17:21:25.299079511Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3p6e","title":"[Harmonize] Migrate orphan sub-tasks to hierarchical IDs","description":"# [Harmonize] Migrate orphan sub-tasks to hierarchical IDs

## Purpose
Ensure all sub-tasks use hierarchical IDs following the {epic-id}.{n}.{m} pattern.

## Orphans to Migrate
- asupersync-8z9 → under n5o or xrc
- asupersync-ior → under n5o or xrc
- asupersync-9d3 → under n5o or xrc
- asupersync-c61 → under n5o or xrc

## Hierarchical ID Pattern
- EPIC: asupersync-{id}
- Sub-EPIC: asupersync-{epic}.{n}
- Task: asupersync-{epic}.{n}.{m}

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All orphan sub-tasks linked to parent EPICs
- [ ] New beads use hierarchical ID pattern
- [ ] `br show {epic}` shows complete child tree
- [ ] No floating sub-tasks without parent dependencies

## Verification
```bash
bv --robot-suggest | jq '.suggestions | map(select(.type == "orphan"))'
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:34.679412816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:31:36.812205071Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:31:36.812110173Z","close_reason":"Linked orphan tasks to asupersync-xrc as parent-child (8z9/ior/9d3/c61); verified no cycles","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3p6e","depends_on_id":"bd-zs64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"bd-3p6e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added parent-child deps to asupersync-xrc for legacy tasks: asupersync-8z9, asupersync-ior, asupersync-9d3, asupersync-c61. Verified no cycles.","created_at":"2026-01-23T02:31:29Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3qc6","title":"Cancel-Aware Sync Primitives Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E)","description":"# Cancel-Aware Sync Primitives Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for cancel-aware synchronization primitives (q48) ensuring correctness under cancellation and two-phase permit semantics.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Mutex: lock, try_lock, cancel-during-wait
- RwLock: read, write, upgrade, downgrade
- Semaphore: acquire, try_acquire, two-phase permits
- Barrier: wait, reset
- Notify: notify_one, notify_all, notify_waiters
- OnceCell: get_or_init, get_or_try_init

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Mutex contention | Fair queuing, no starvation |
| RwLock readers | Concurrent read access |
| RwLock writer priority | Writer preference |
| Semaphore permits | Count accuracy |
| Semaphore two-phase | Reserve/commit/abort |
| Barrier sync | All tasks reach barrier |
| Notify wake ordering | FIFO wake semantics |
| OnceCell race | Single initialization |

### 3. Cancel-Safety Tests
- Cancel while waiting for mutex: no leak
- Cancel while holding permit: proper release
- Cancel during barrier wait: barrier reset
- Cancel during semaphore reserve: abort semantics

### 4. Stress Tests
- High contention scenarios
- Many waiters, few resources
- Rapid acquire/release cycles
- Mixed read/write patterns

### 5. Deadlock Detection Tests
- Cycle detection in lock graphs
- Priority inversion scenarios
- Lab runtime deadlock oracle

## Logging Requirements
- Lock events logged with task IDs
- On failure: dump waiter queues, lock state
- Contention metrics: wait times, queue lengths

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All primitives have unit tests
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for all operations
- [ ] No deadlocks in stress tests
- [ ] Two-phase permits work correctly
- [ ] `cargo test sync` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib sync
cargo test --test sync_e2e
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"MagentaReef","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:47:57.306837602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:34:21.891711941Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3qc6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-q48","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3sfb","title":"Fix test files using old synchronous channel API","description":"Tests need to be updated to use try_send() instead of the async send().await. Affected test files include net_unix, phase0_verification, stream_e2e, and inline tests in channel/mpsc.rs, stream/mod.rs, etc. The channel API changed from sync to async so tests that call send() synchronously need to use try_send() instead.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldGate","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:49:42.627917220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T19:16:31.444115334Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T19:16:31.444039493Z","close_reason":"Tests compile and pass.  {"id":"bd-js88","title":"Async Filesystem Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, fault injection)","description":"# Async Filesystem Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for async filesystem operations (aqy, tokio-fs equivalent) ensuring cancel-safety and data integrity.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- File: open, create, read, write, seek
- File: sync_all, sync_data, set_len
- Directory: create_dir, create_dir_all, remove_dir
- Directory: read_dir, canonicalize
- Metadata: metadata, symlink_metadata
- Permissions: set_permissions
- Links: hard_link, symlink, read_link
- Remove: remove_file, remove_dir_all
- Rename: rename
- Copy: copy

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Read entire file | Basic file I/O |
| Write + sync | Durability semantics |
| Append to file | Concurrent append safety |
| Cancel mid-write | Two-phase write safety |
| Directory traversal | Recursive listing |
| Large file copy | Streaming copy |
| Atomic rename | POSIX rename semantics |
| Temp file workflow | Create, write, rename |

### 3. Fault Injection Tests
- ENOENT: File not found
- EACCES: Permission denied
- ENOSPC: No space left
- EINTR: Interrupted
- Partial write (disk full mid-write)
- Concurrent file access

### 4. Two-Phase Tests
- Reserve write buffer
- Commit on success
- Abort on cancel/error
- No partial writes visible

## Logging Requirements
- File events logged with file paths and handles
- On failure: dump I/O operation state
- Byte counts: read, written, synced

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All file operations have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass under lab runtime
- [ ] Fault injection tests verify error handling
- [ ] Two-phase semantics enforced
- [ ] `cargo test fs` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib fs
cargo test --test fs_e2e
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:48:45.105825582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:07:18.380959143Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:07:18.380894183Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-js88","depends_on_id":"asupersync-aqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-kh65","title":"[Harmonize] Standardize EPIC naming convention","description":"# [Harmonize] Standardize EPIC naming convention

## Purpose
Adopt consistent [EPIC-{category}] naming across all EPICs.

## Categories
- PHASE: Phase 0-5 core runtime EPICs
- TOKIO: Tokio ecosystem equivalents
- INFRA: Tooling and infrastructure
- APP: Application-level features

## Current Inconsistencies
- `[EPIC]` prefix
- `EPIC:` prefix  
- `Epic #N` format
- `[SUB-EPIC]` prefix

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 26+ EPICs follow [EPIC-{category}] format
- [ ] Sub-EPICs use [SUB-EPIC-{category}] or hierarchical IDs
- [ ] `br list --type=epic` shows consistent naming
- [ ] No ambiguous or confusing titles

## Verification
```bash
br list --type=epic | grep -E "^\w+ asupersync"  # Check naming patterns
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:25.325303353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:34:31.350280324Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:34:31.350199592Z","close_reason":"Completed: All 33 EPICs and 3 SUB-EPICs now follow consistent [EPIC-{category}] and [SUB-EPIC-{category}] naming convention. Categories: PHASE(6), TOKIO(14), INFRA(12), APP(1). Triage + prioritize fixes (focus on critical first).","notes":"Ran UBS on src/. Critical: std::thread::spawn without join in time/sleep.rs and transport/mock.rs. Large inventory of panic!/unwrap/expect (existing) to triage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-29T04:21:11.924845036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T16:43:36.479646188Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T16:43:36.479555880Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-obpv","title":"Time/Timers Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, determinism)","description":"# Time/Timers Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the time/timer infrastructure (mf6) ensuring virtual/wall time correctness, budget integration, and deterministic behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- sleep(duration): basic pause
- sleep_until(instant): absolute time pause
- interval(period): repeating timer
- interval_at(start, period): explicit start
- MissedTickBehavior: Burst, Delay, Skip
- Timer heap operations: insert, pop, cancel

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Sleep + cancel | Clean cancellation, Cancelled outcome |
| Nested timeouts | Budget propagation |
| Interval tick counting | Correct tick delivery |
| Missed tick burst | Catch-up behavior |
| Concurrent timers | No timer starvation |
| Budget exhaustion | Deadline enforcement |

### 3. Virtual vs Wall Time Tests
- Lab runtime: virtual time advances only on explicit call
- Production: wall time advances independently
- Cross-mode: same test logic, different time source

### 4. Determinism Tests
- Same seed → identical timer ordering
- Replay tests: record and replay timer sequences
- No wall-clock leakage in lab mode

## Logging Requirements
- Timer events logged with virtual/wall timestamps
- On failure: dump timer heap state, pending timers
- Deadline violations logged with budget context

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All timer operations have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass under both lab and production runtime
- [ ] Determinism verified via replay tests
- [ ] Budget integration tests pass
- [ ] `cargo test time` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib time
cargo test --test time_e2e
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"OpusSession","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:46:51.972964834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T06:02:26.467521125Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-obpv","depends_on_id":"asupersync-mf6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-q0om","title":"[Harmonize] Merge I/O EPICs: 90l9 → ds8","description":"# [Harmonize] Merge I/O EPICs: 90l9 → ds8

## Purpose
Migrate all unique sub-EPICs from asupersync-90l9 into asupersync-ds8 hierarchy, then close 90l9.

## Sub-EPICs to Migrate
- wx8h: Core Reactor
- l92b: epoll
- 3z8z: kqueue  
- 7tk3: Lab Reactor
- ui2r: TCP/UDP
- ycir: UDS
- 56fs: Integration Testing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 90l9 sub-EPICs linked to ds8 hierarchy via dependencies
- [ ] 90l9 closed with migration note referencing ds8
- [ ] No orphaned sub-EPICs after merge
- [ ] ds8 description updated to reflect absorbed scope
- [ ] `br show asupersync-ds8` shows correct dependent tree

## Verification
```bash
br show asupersync-90l9  # Should be closed
br show asupersync-ds8   # Should show all I/O sub-EPICs
bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'  # No cycles
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ClaudeOpus45","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:16.599858279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:23:32.548968635Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:23:32.548896109Z","close_reason":"Verified: 90l9 already merged into ds8 hierarchy. All sub-EPICs (wx8h, l92b, 3z8z, 7tk3, ui2r, ycir) are closed. Remaining work (56fs, 5w2z, 8jx5) migrated to ds8.4 and ds8.3. No cycles detected.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-q0om","depends_on_id":"bd-zs64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zs64","title":"[EPIC-INFRA] Bead Harmonization - API & Agent Interface Coherence","description":"# [EPIC-INFRA] Bead Harmonization - API & Agent Interface Coherence

## Purpose
Master epic tracking bead harmonization: merge duplicates, standardize naming, fix priorities, migrate orphan sub-tasks. NO FEATURE LOSS.

## Sub-Tasks
1. bd-q0om: Merge I/O EPICs (90l9 → ds8)
2. bd-hzrb: Merge Parallel Runtime EPICs (n5o → xrc)
3. bd-kh65: Standardize EPIC naming convention
4. bd-4rpn: Fix priority alignments
5. bd-3p6e: Migrate orphan sub-tasks to hierarchical IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All duplicate EPICs merged or closed with migration notes
- [ ] All EPICs follow [EPIC-{category}] naming convention
- [ ] Priority follows dependency order (parents ≤ children)
- [ ] All sub-tasks use hierarchical IDs ({epic}.{n}.{m})
- [ ] No cycles in dependency graph (verified via `bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'`)
- [ ] `bv --robot-triage` returns healthy project metrics

## Verification Commands
```bash
bv --robot-triage | jq '.quick_ref'
bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'
br list --status=closed | grep "DUPLICATE\|MIGRATED"
```

## Logging Strategy
- All changes logged in docs/bead-harmonization-migration.md
- Each bead update includes reason in description
- Git commit messages reference bead IDs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:27:46.833876766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:50.988353887Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:15:13.406855498Z","close_reason":"All harmonization sub-tasks closed; naming/priority alignment verified; orphans migrated; no dependency cycles","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-zs64","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Checked sub-tasks: bd-q0om/bd-hzrb/bd-kh65/bd-4rpn/bd-3p6e all closed.
